title: Blended Visa Office-Referred Program: About the process - Canada.ca

## Blended Visa Office-Referred Program
- 1. About the program
- 2. Who can apply to sponsor a refugee

- 4. After you apply to sponsor a refugee

## How the BVOR program works
No endorsement of any products or services is expressed or implied.
Current position: 00:00:00
Total time: 00:02:10
Find out how the BVOR program benefits both sponsors and refugees.
The Blended Visa Office-Referred (BVOR) Program gives Canadians and permanent residents a chance to play a key role in helping refugees in need of resettlement. Through this program, Canadian citizens and permanent residents can
- become private sponsors and
- get matched with refugees identified by a referral organization, like the United Nations Refugee Agency (UNHCR) .
To help refugees start their new lives in Canada, private sponsors commit to providing
- 6 months of financial support and
- 12 months of social, emotional and settlement support
The Government of Canada provides the other 6 months of financial support.

## Why sponsor through BVOR?
Sponsoring through the BVOR program
- invigorates and enhances our communities and
- allows Canadians to have a direct impact on their communities contribute to global efforts to help refugees in need of protection
- have a direct impact on their communities
- contribute to global efforts to help refugees in need of protection
Find out if you can sponsor through BVOR
Apply to become a BVOR sponsor
Get help from the Refugee Sponsorship Training Program
